hook: ChatGPT gave me a 9-step plan when I asked how to fix my morning routine. I needed 2 sentences.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 9-step plan when I asked how to fix my morning routine. I needed 2 sentences.

This is the most common AI frustration nobody talks about.

It's not that the answer was wrong. It was thorough, well-organized, probably helpful — for someone else.

But you're a "just tell me the answer" person. You've known this about yourself forever. Your notes app is three words per bullet. Your texts are famously short. You've left voicemails exactly zero times in your adult life.

The AI doesn't know any of that.

So it defaults to the universal version of helpful — which is: comprehensive, structured, and completely exhausting to read if your brain works the way yours does.

There are 8 distinct thinking styles we've mapped at bootfile.ai. The "Direct Processor" is one of them. Give them a listicle and they'll close the tab. Give them a single sharp insight and they'll actually use it.

The fix isn't prompting harder. It's the AI knowing you before the conversation starts.

That's what a BootFile does.

One profile. Every tool. Finally talks to you like it gets you.

Link in bio to build yours (takes 4 minutes, asks you weird questions in the best way).
